model_narrative: We plan a surveillance flight for an aircraft that must scan a collection of swaths, where each swath is a region scanned by flying through it between one of its valid pairs of entry and exit nodes. The aircraft flies a single tour that enters and leaves each swath once, and we decide which arcs to fly between swath node combinations and the resulting order in which the swaths are visited. Each arc has a known flight distance. The objective is to minimize the total flight distance of the tour.

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the swaths, which are the regions that must be scanned; the first swath serves as the start and end region of the tour and the remaining swaths are the regions to be visited

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the candidate entry and exit nodes of a swath; each swath is scanned by traversing it between one of its valid node combinations

sets.name: a
sets.members: the valid arcs, listed as four-tuples (from-swath, from-node, to-swath, to-node)
sets.doc: the set of valid arcs that can be flown; each arc connects a node combination of one swath to a node combination of a different swath, so an arc is given as a from-swath, a from-node, a to-swath, and a to-node, and no arc stays within the same swath

sets.name: sx
sets.members: the valid (swath, node) combinations that appear as the origin endpoint of some arc
sets.doc: the valid swath-node combinations, meaning the (swath, node) pairs that are actually usable as endpoints of flown arcs; a (swath, node) pair not in this set is never a valid place to enter or leave

params.name: l
params.doc: the flight distance of each arc, that is the length flown when traveling from the arc's origin swath-node combination to its destination swath-node combination, in distance units

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the arc-use indicator; equals 1 if that arc is flown as part of the tour and 0 otherwise

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the swath-ordering indicator over an ordered pair of swaths; equals 1 if the second swath is visited immediately after the first swath in the tour and 0 otherwise; only meaningful for pairs of distinct swaths

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total length of the chosen tour, in distance units

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every swath must be entered exactly once by the tour. For each swath, count the flown arcs whose destination lies in that swath across all of its node combinations, and require that this count equals one.

constraints.name: sx:
constraints.intent: The flight must be continuous at every usable swath-node combination, so the aircraft leaves a combination exactly when it arrives there. For each valid swath-node combination, the number of flown arcs arriving at that combination must equal the number of flown arcs departing from it. Combinations that are not valid endpoints impose no such requirement.

constraints.name: y[i,
constraints.intent: The order in which swaths are visited follows from the arcs actually flown between them. For each ordered pair of distinct swaths, the indicator that the second swath is visited immediately after the first must equal the total number of flown arcs that go from any node combination of the first swath to any node combination of the second.

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: The total tour length accumulates the distance of every flown arc. Set the total tour length equal to the sum over all arcs of each arc's flight distance counted only when that arc is flown.

query: We're curious how tied the plan is to bouncing straight back and forth between swaths s2 and s4. Suppose we don't let the aircraft both fly s2-then-s4 and s4-then-s2 in the same mission — at most one of those two direct hops. What total distance does the optimizer return?

query: Let's look at the s5 / s7 pair the same way — what does the plan cost if we rule out the aircraft shuttling directly both ways between s5 and s7, keeping at most one of those two legs?

query: I'm looking at this plan and the aircraft just ping-pongs between s6 and s8 — out to s8, straight back to s6. That's not a real surveillance tour, it's a loop. Shouldn't we forbid taking both the s6-to-s8 and s8-to-s6 hops so it has to connect somewhere else? What is the model seeing that makes that little loop attractive?

query: Same complaint over at s9 and s11 — the route closes a tiny two-swath loop there instead of threading through the area. Couldn't we just ban doing both s9-to-s11 and s11-to-s9, so at least one of those swaths links onward? Why is the optimizer happy parking the aircraft in that loop?

query: The s18 / s20 pairing is yet another of these closed two-swath shuttles. It can't be right that the best mission plan is a bunch of isolated back-and-forths. Shouldn't we at least stop the aircraft from flying both directions between s18 and s20? What's the tradeoff the model is making by keeping that loop?
